name: Curried Eggplant Strudel
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Frozen phyllo dough (24 Sheets) -- defrosted
recipeInstructions:
1. Toss the eggplant with salt and set aside for 30 minutes. Wrap in a towel and squeeze out any excess moisture.
2. In a skillet, sauté the onion in olive oil over medium heat until soft, about 2 minutes. Add garlic, curry powder, cumin, and chili powder; cook another 2 minutes.
3. Add reserved eggplant, tomatoes, sugar, and saffron mixed into vinegar to the mixture. Simmer for 15 minutes or until eggplant is soft. Add water if needed.
4. Add peanuts and let cool completely.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
6. Place a sheet of phyllo on a clean work surface, drizzle lightly with clarified butter. Layer five more sheets, adding more butter atop each layer. Brush top layer with butter.
7. Place a quarter of the filling over the sheets, mounding along one longer side. Roll the sheets into a jelly roll-style starting at the edge with mounded filling. Place seam-side down on a greased sheet pan and brush with more clarified butter.
8. Make three more strudels using remaining phyllo, filling, and butter.
9. Bake in preheated oven for 25-30 minutes until golden brown. Flip pans front to back and top to bottom halfway through. Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
10. Cut the strudel into 1-inch-thick slices and top with yogurt.
